↩️ Reply to OSINT Visioner ‼️ ‼️ 🇯🇵 BREAKING - The Japanese government has approved a major relaxation of arms export rules, effectively ending the post-war pacifism era and opening the country's military-industrial complex to the global market. Japan, with a $60 billion military budget, already produces high-tech systems — ranging from submarines and warships to its own missiles, tanks, and combat aircraft. Corporations like Toshiba and Mitsubishi Electric are beginning massive hiring and factory expansions, expecting defense sales to grow by more than 50% by 2031. As a first practical step, Tokyo plans to supply second-hand missile frigates to the Philippines to strengthen the balance against China in the South China Sea, as well as joint projects with Poland in the fields of electronic warfare and anti-drone systems. Additionally, Ukraine is expected to join this initiative: an industrial group is already being formed in Tokyo to combine the efforts of Japanese and Ukrainian drone manufacturers. Although the ban on supplying weapons to active war zones remains, the formation of supply chains independent from the U.S. in Asia makes Japan a serious competitor, including for South Korea and Israel. At the same time, the country's relatively unique and self-sufficient technological base allows it to quickly take a position in the niche of air defense components and systems, offering allies an alternative to the overburdened U.S. defense industry. See the latest updates with us: @visionergeo tweet

The race has narrowed to three candidates: incumbent Mohammed Shia al‑Sudani, Nouri al‑Maliki, and Basim al‑Badri. A candidate needs eight out of twelve votes from framework leaders. Maliki is reportedly willing to withdraw only if Badri becomes the nominee, and does not want Sudani to be chosen. @wfwitness

🇺🇸 🇻🇦 The US Conference of Catholic Bishops has released a response to JD Vance's comments about Pope Leo XIV: 'For over a thousand years, the Catholic Church has taught just war theory and it is that long tradition the Holy Father carefully references in his comments on war. A constant tenet of that thousand-year tradition is a nation can only legitimately take up the sword ‘in self-defense, once all peace efforts have failed’ That is, to be a just war it must be a defense against another who actively wages war, which is what the Holy Father actually said: ‘He does not listen to the prayers of those who wage war. When Pope Leo XIV speaks as supreme pastor of the universal Church, he is not merely offering opinions on theology, he is preaching the Gospel and exercising his ministry as the Vicar of Christ. The consistent teaching of the Church is insistent that all people of good will must pray and work toward lasting peace while avoiding the evils and injustices that accompany all wars' 🔴 @DDGeopolitics

⚡️ 🇮🇷 🇴🇲 🇺🇸 🇮🇱 Reuters : Iran has proposed allowing ships to sail freely through the Omani side of the Strait of Hormuz without risk of attack, as part of negotiations with the United States to prevent renewed conflict. The source didn't specify whether Iran would also agree to help clear mines in the Strait or if all ships, including Israeli ships would be allowed to pass freely. They did add that the proposal hinged on whether the U.S. is ready to meet Iran's demands. @wfwitness

Open Source Intel Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent on the financial war against Iran: "What may prove to be fatal mistakes the Iranians made was bombing their neighbors. Who are now willing to be much more transparent in terms of the funds, or do a deeper dive in investigating the funds that are held within their banking systems." "So, we have pushed out to them the request that we want to freeze more funds of the leadership of the IRGC and any members of Iranian leadership." "The other thing that we have done is we have told companies, we have told countries that if you are buying Iranian oil, that if Iranian money is sitting in your banks, we are now willing to apply secondary sanctions, which is a very stern measure." "The Iranians should know that this is going to be the financial equivalent of what we saw in the kinetic activities." tweet

🇱🇧 The Hand that Gives is Above the Hand that Takes Yesterday the International Monetary Fund (world bank) stated a $1 billion dollar rapid relief loan was prepared for Lebanon. Such an amount would help the Lebanese government tackle many financial issues. But what were the conditions? One of the conditions of this loan was the Lebanese government’s role in restoring order and authority in the state. It is no coincidence in the timing between the sit down in DC and the IMF loan. But what is important to know is the Lebanese state no longer speaks on behalf of Hezbollah and its right to resist an invading and occupying force. How can a state even discuss or consider a limited ceasefire when an opposing force is deep within its territory having killed thousands of its citizens. Such a government and its statesmen are only surrendering to save whatever remains of its territory and fill their own pockets. So how is Israel benefitting from the talks of a ceasefire? A temporary ceasefire in Lebanon would only serve Israel 2 major benefits 1. Time to reinforce thousands of Israeli troops into southern Lebanon 2. Increase of hostilities between the Lebanese state and Hezbollah, knowing Hezbollah will not comply with such agreement Hezbollah’s failure to comply with any agreement would now be personal with the Lebanese state. Instability on Lebanon’s end would cancel the $1 billion dollar deal with the IMF (world bank)

↩️ Reply to DD Geopolitics 🇷🇺 “Sleep well, European partners.” The list of European drone production sites for Ukraine is effectively a list of potential Russian strike targets, Dmitry Medvedev said. 🔴 @DDGeopolitics

🇷🇺 Russia’s Defense Ministry released locations of European, Israeli, and Turkish facilities tied to Ukrainian companies producing drones used for strikes against Russia. Key points from the MoD statement: ➡️ Several European countries, facing Ukrainian battlefield setbacks in late March, decided to scale up drone production and deliveries to Kiev. ➡️ Expansion is being driven by increased funding for Ukrainian and joint ventures operating in Europe. ➡️ Moscow views these moves as a deliberate step toward sharp military-political escalation. ➡️ Using Europe-based production to support drone attacks on Russia risks unpredictable consequences. ➡️ European governments are, in Moscow’s view, steadily drawing their countries deeper into the conflict. ➡️ Some European states are becoming a de facto strategic rear for Ukraine. ➡️ European publics should understand the security risks and be aware of the locations of these drone production sites. 🔴 @DDGeopolitics

↩️ Reply to WarFront Witness ⚡️ 🇺🇸 🇮🇷 🇱🇧 The United States has imposed sanctions on an allegedly extensive illicit oil smuggling network operated allegedly by senior Iranian elites, targeting individuals, companies and vessels that allegedly generate revenue for Tehran and its proxies. The designations include also include: Chetan Prakash Balhotra, an Indian national based in the UAE. Seyed Naiemaei Badroddin Moosavi, a allegedly Hezbollah‑linked financier operating from the UK, UAE and Turkey. The Office of Foreign Asset Control also blacklisted the following more than a dozen shipping firms and entities: ACS Global BV in the Netherlands ACS Trading LLC and Lotus Universal LLC in Dubai Several Marshall Islands‑registered shipping companies such as Anika Lines Inc., Aura Lines Inc. and Nardie International S.A. Eight oil tankers – including the Anaya, Anika, Aura, Bellaris, Cauveri, Daphne V, Horae, Silvar and Versa – were also designated for their role in allegedly transporting Iranian oil. The action targets Iran’s oil export revenues that fund its military programmes and support for Hezbollah. @wfwitness

↩️ Reply to WarFront Witness: "⚡️🇺🇸🇮🇷The U.S. Treasury announced that they’re aggressively moving forward with Economic Fury. They advised financial institutions that they will be leveraging the full range of tools they have to deploy secondary sanctions against financial institutions…" ⚡️ 🇺🇸 🇮🇷 🇱🇧 The U.S. Treasury has sanctioned Mohammad Hossein Shamkhani, son of former senior Iranian official Ali Shamkhani, targeting Iran’s oil transportation network as the first sanction under Economic Fury. The U.S. Treasury said it sanctioned more than two dozen individuals, companies, and vessels involved in what it described as a large-scale network moving Iranian oil and generating billions of dollars in revenue for Tehran and its allies. The network uses front companies across multiple countries to manage shipping, logistics, and financial operations while masking its activities. Several vessels accused of transporting Iranian and Russian petroleum products were also designated as blocked property. The Treasury added that the measures also target a financial network allegedly involved in exchanging Iranian oil for Venezuelan gold, which it said helped fund groups including Hezbollah and Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps. @wfwitness

↩️ Reply to WarFront Witness: "⚡️🇺🇸🇮🇷🇷🇺U.S. Treasury Secretary Bessent confirmed that the U.S. will not renew waivers on Iranian and Russia oil. "We will not be renewing the general license on Russian oil, and we will not be renewing the general license on Iranian oil. Because, again…" ⚡️ 🇺🇸 🇮🇷 U.S. Press Secretary Leavitt's response to a question on the timeline for the blockade of the Strait of Hormuz: "Sure. I will never set timelines on behalf of the president of The United States.
